# TLDR Review: Spindrift Sparkling Water Variety Pack

By ShopSavvy Team

Published: January 23rd, 2026

### Pros

- •The pack size of 20 cans with equal distribution of each flavor offers good value and variety to stock your fridge.
- •Ideal for people who want a sparkling water that’s unsweetened and authentic in its fruit flavor.
- •The brand is trusted for balancing fizz and natural fruit taste without an overly tart or artificial experience.
- •Each flavor has its own distinct profile, preventing flavor fatigue.
- •Great substitute for soda, juice, or generic seltzers, making it versatile for casual drinking or as a mixer in cocktails.
- •Flavors are subtle and more noticeable at room temperature, but remain pleasantly refreshing when chilled.
- •The variety pack includes four distinct flavors—Raspberry Lime, Grapefruit, Orange Mango, and Lemon—giving a nice flavor range for different occasions.
- •Provides a light, crisp, and refreshing sparkling water option that’s perfect for those avoiding sugar and additives.
- •Made with real squeezed fruit, offering a clean and natural taste without any artificial flavors or sweeteners.

### Cons

- •Because the flavors come solely from real fruit juice with no sweeteners, some consumers find the taste less vibrant or less sweet compared to other sparkling water brands.
- •At times, certain flavors outside Lemon, like Grapefruit or Orange Mango, have been described as "weird" or not quite matching expectations.
- •The flavors overall tend to be on the tart side and may be too strong or sharp for those who prefer milder, more subtle tastes.
- •Some flavors, especially Lemon, can taste a bit medicinal or Alkeltzer-like, which might not appeal to everyone.

### Bottom Line

If you’re looking for a sparkling water that truly emphasizes natural fruit flavor without added sugars, artificial additives, or sweeteners, Spindrift’s 4 Flavor Variety Pack is a refreshing, authentic choice. Just keep in mind the tartness level might be a bit much if you want something mild or candy-like. If you prefer a gentler or sweeter sparkling water option, you might want to explore other brands that balance natural flavor with subtle sweetness. Otherwise, t

results.title: Spindrift Review: A New Flavored Sparkling Water
results.text:
Spindrift Review: A New Flavored Sparkling Water

# Spindrift Review: A New Flavored Sparkling Water

This Spindrift review is for all of you who want something special to drink without too much sugar.

I have been on the hunt for ways to enjoy water and have it remind you of soda (or beer). One of my favorite posts is Top Eight Tips for Tastier Water for ideas on how to drink more water.

Bubly sparkling water has been my favorite lately for a flavored water in a can.

Spindrift may have unseated Bubly. My daughter brought this home on a trip to Target and I was intrigued. I have banished soda from the house (except for special occasions) and we love trying new things.

Do you see that? 1/4 of a real pineapple! Spindrift’s ingredients include carbonated water, pineapple juice, and citric acid. That is it. It has 15 calories per can.

The concept is simple. A little bit of real juice in a big can of water. Unlike Bubly, which is the essence of a flavor and zero calories - Spindrift meets us with some juice.

How does it taste? Maybe because I never drink soda I am biased - but it tastes like soda. It is amazing how far 15 calories of juice can take you.

Grapefruit! YES! Do you know that most of the grapefruit flavored sparkling waters taste like soap? I love grapefruit so I have been disappointed many times.

Spindrift grapefruit tastes like grapefruit. The ingredients include carbonated water, grapefruit juice, orange juice, lemon juice, and hibiscus.

They probably add the orange juice so it isn’t too sour. I thought it was perfect.

Blackberry was another excellent flavor.

I share Spindrift because it is nice to crack open a can of something at around 5 PM that you look forward to drinking. It isn’t alcohol or soda but it doesn’t feel like a compromise.

The Spindrift current flavors are pineapple, lime, cranberry raspberry, lemon and black tea, strawberry, grapefruit, blackberry, cucumber, lemon, orange mango, raspberry lime.

My favorite Spindrift flavor is grapefruit but all three of them were really good.

Have you tried Sprindrift? What is your review? Thanks for sharing!

---

#### Other posts you might like:

---

I went on a beach vacation this year to North Carolina. We rented a house so we could social distance.....

---

results.title: Why Spindrift's Peach Strawberry Flavor Is The Absolute Worst
results.text:
Why Spindrift's Peach Strawberry Flavor Is The Absolute Worst

# The Worst Spindrift Sparkling Water Flavor, According To Our Taste Test

By Mathieson Nishimura June 7, 2025 7:00 pm EST

PJ McDonnell/Shutterstock

Spindrift has taken the internet by storm lately. In a sea of pretty, mid-tasting sparkling waters, it stands out with bold, brash flavors that we love to see. But as with any brand, Spindrift has some drinks that knock it out of the park and some that leave us underwhelmed. In our list of 10 Spindrift sparkling water flavors, ranked worst to best, the peach strawberry flavor landed at the bottom of the list. With overly saccharine peach notes and nearly sour strawberry, you're getting a confusing flavor experience that will leave you wishing you'd grabbed another can.

What about this flavor makes it a dud? To us, the peach notes are almost too ripe. With a modest ingredients list of sparkling water, peach puree, strawberry puree, citric acid, and sugar, that extreme sweetness overwhelms the palate. It also makes it difficult to pick up on the strawberry, which tastes under-ripe by comparison, bringing a tartness that might have complemented the peach's sweetness well, but just tastes like a flavor cacophony. While it's not the worst thing ever, it's nowhere near Spindrift's best offering.

## How Spindrift's peach strawberry stacks up to other flavors

Samantha Maxwell/Tasting Table

We've talked about how the worst Ramune soda tastes disappointingly artificial, and that flavor was peach. Artificial peach flavors can just be misses for some people. Since Spindrift uses peach puree rather than artificial flavoring, it should presumably lack some of that strange, artificial taste that haters of peach candies and soju reel at. But tragically, using real peach potentially adds to the problem of it tasting overly ripe and sickeningly sweet. Compare that to our top-ranked Spindrift flavor, Island Punch, which has the perfect balance of tropical fruit for a refreshing, not-too-sweet sipping experience, and you'll see exactly why peach strawberry sunk so low.

If you're still craving peach or strawberry sodas, there's one Poppi soda flavor we'd buy over and over again, and it's the strawberry lemon. This refreshing drink gives you a perfect tart-sweet blend that the peach-strawberry Spindrift flavor accidentally stumbles over. The other Spindrift flavors also pack a good punch, and we're particularly fond of the citrus flavors like lemon, grapefruit, and raspberry lime. If you do end up with peach strawberry Spindrift and it's not for you, try drinking it straight from the fridge. The super crisp and cold temperature helps cut down on that mushy, over-ripe peach vibe, so you might find the can more enjoyable. Then, once you're done, you can get yourself back to the store to pick up another flavor and get the real Spindrift experience.

## Recommended

results.title: All 19 Spindrift Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Taste Test Guide
results.author: RON SILBERSTEIN
results.text:
All 19 Spindrift Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Taste Test Guide

Spindrift flavors ranked from worst to best – that is exactly what I set out to discover after noticing this sparkling water brand everywhere from grocery stores to office refrigerators. Unlike typical sparkling waters that use natural flavors (a vague term that often means artificial), Spindrift stands apart by using real fruit juice and fruit purees in every can.

Our team spent three weeks testing 19 different Spindrift varieties. We tasted them chilled, at room temperature, and even as cocktail mixers. Some flavors blew us away with their authenticity. Others missed the mark entirely. I will walk you through every single flavor, explain why each ranks where it does, and help you avoid wasting money on the duds.

By the end of this guide, you will know exactly which Spindrift flavors deserve space in your refrigerator and which ones you should leave on the shelf.

Table of Contents

Toggle

## Our Taste Testing Methodology

Before diving into the rankings, let me explain how we evaluated each flavor. All testing occurred in our office kitchen over three weeks in March 2026. We purchased every flavor available at three major retailers to ensure we did not miss any varieties.

Each flavor underwent testing at two temperatures: straight from the refrigerator at 38 degrees Fahrenheit, and after sitting out for 30 minutes to simulate a beach or poolside scenario. We evaluated five criteria for every can: flavor accuracy (does it taste like the actual fruit), sweetness level, carbonation intensity, refreshment factor, and overall drinkability. Multiple team members scored each flavor independently, and we averaged the results to create these rankings.

One important note: Spindrift contains real juice, which means the flavors taste different from artificially-flavored sparkling waters like LaCroix. Some people love this intensity. Others find it overwhelming. Keep your personal preferences in mind as you read these rankings.

## The Bottom Tier: Flavors to Skip

Let us start with the flavors that disappointed us. These ranked at the bottom of our taste test for various reasons – some tasted artificial despite the real juice claim, others had strange aftertastes, and a few simply lacked the refreshment factor we want from sparkling water.

### 19. Nojito

Nojito attempts to capture the essence of a mojito without the alcohol. The concept sounds appealing, but the execution falls flat. The mint flavor dominates aggressively, overwhelming any lime notes present. Several testers described the aftertaste as reminiscent of chewing gum left in a hot car.

The real problem with Nojito is the imbalance. Real mojitos achieve harmony between mint, lime, and sweetness. This canned version tips too far toward the herbal mint side, creating something that feels more like a mouthwash than a refreshing beverage. Skip this one unless you absolutely love intense mint flavors.

### 18. Cosmopolitan

The Co

results.title: I Tried Spindrift Sparkling Water For The First Time - BuzzFeed
results.author: Abigail Lauren
results.text:
I Tried Spindrift Sparkling Water For The First Time

by Abigail LaurenCommunity ContributorPost approved and edited by BuzzFeed Community Team

## Hi folks! My name is Abigail, and over quarantine, I have become a bit of sparkling water professional.

> View this photo on Instagram

instagram.com/ Via Instagram: @abigailsaathoff

## Spindrift is very different than all other sparkling waters I've tried, because Spindrift is made ONLY with sparkling water and fruit juice.

Getty Images/Target

I have seemingly tried a little bit of every brand, flavor, and type of seltzer. So, when I saw Spindrift in the aisle for the first time, I thought it was only fair that I gave it a go.

## Spindrift offers 11 flavors including grapefruit, lemon, pineapple, lime, cranberry raspberry, and cucumber — just to name a few.

> View this photo on Instagram

instagram.com

## When I picked up Spindrift for the first time, I was intrigued and excited to try it. I grabbed a variety pack with 30 cans and three different flavors, including lemon, raspberry lime, and grapefruit.

> View this photo on Instagram

instagram.com

## But once I had actually paid for it and took it home I was skeptical. Do I really like the taste of real fruit juice? Is that what I'm really looking for in a sparkling water? What if it's gross, and I wasted my money?

Tap to play GIF

Bravo

## Then I figured... I'll just go for it!

Canva

## The first flavor I tried was the Lemon, which again *only* contains lemon juice and carbonated water, adding up to a measly three calories.

> View this photo on Instagram

instagram.com

Canva

This was the flavor I was most excited about, because I assumed you can't go wrong with lemon. At first I was a bit taken aback by the extremely strong lemon flavor and sourness, which I probably should have expected. It took a couple of sips to get over the initial sourness, but after that, it had a just had a bit of a great bite, and was extremely easy to drink.

Canva/Apple

Overall, I would rate it a 7/10.

The lemon flavor was great, but my sensitive palate couldn't seem to get over the sourness. I would still drink this flavor over any other sparkling water brand's lemon flavor.

## Would I suggest it to a friend?

Yes! Overall, I really liked the lemon flavor, and definitely preferred it to some of the other lemon sparkling waters I've tried, but it definitely wasn't my favorite of the three. I would definitely drink another one, but it wouldn't be my first choice.

Canva / Getty Images

## The second flavor I tried was the Raspberry Lime. This flavor consists of carbonated water, raspberry purée, raspberry juice, and lime juice. This all adds up to only nine calories.

Spindrift on Instagram / Via instagram.com

Canva

I was excited but skeptical about this flavor. I love raspberries and limes, but wasn't quite sure about the idea of them with no sweeteners. Upon the first sip, I really enjoyed it. The raspberry flavor tasted just like biting into a fre

results.title: Spindrift Spiked Review: Spiced Apple Cider
results.text:
Spindrift Spiked Review: Spiced Apple Cider

# Spindrift Just Released A Brand New Hard Seltzer Flavor — Here's What I LOVED And What I Wish Was Different

Spindrift now makes a regular sparkling apple cider flavor and a boozy version. I cracked open an 8-pack of the latter in the middle of the workday for, you know, research.

Raven IshakPublished on November 23, 2022

- Share via sms
- Share via email
- Share via pinterest
- Share via facebook

## When it comes to fall, there are a few things that are synonymous with this time of year. While some may think of apple picking, pumpkin spice lattes, or even crunchy colorful leaves on the ground (bonus points if there's a cute puppy running into the pile), I, on the other hand, try to do two things every fall: 1) an annual celebration of Chris Evans in a cozy sweater from Knives Out, and 2) get cozy and sip some apple cider (either with or without alcohol).

Tap to play GIF

T-street productions / Via giphy.com

## To that second part, you can imagine my delight when I recently heard that Spindrift — aka: my favorite sparkling water brand — was coming out with a new flavor: Spiced Apple Cider.

Spindrift

They offer both regular and spiked versions — and I knew I had to get my hands on the latter.

## But first, let's do a quick refresher on apple cider. Basically, it's a drink that's usually served warm and makes an appearance around the fall time in America. And while there are hundreds of ways to make this delicious beverage, the main ingredients usually consist of apples, cinnamon, whole cloves, allspice, and sugar. And unlike apple juice, it is unfiltered and usually unpasteurized.

Pavelkant / Via Getty Images/iStockphoto

AND if you want to make things boozy, you can also add some alcohol like dark, light, or spiced rum. (If you're interested in making a homemade version for yourself, check out this Tasty recipe here.)

## But since Spindrift has a Spiked Sparkling Water Spiced Apple Cider ready for me to drink — which is made with crushed apples, cinnamon, and alcohol from fermented cane sugar — I figured why get any dishes dirty when I can just pop open a can and call it a day. The drink is 4% alcohol by volume, or slightly less than the average beer (5%).

Raven Ishak

Yes, it was in the middle of the workday. No, I don't have any regrets. It's called research.

## So after receiving an eight-pack precisely for this taste test, I popped one open and first took a sniff directly from the can and was instantly met with an authentic apple juice aroma (aka no artificial "natural flavors" in this, folks) — so I was excited to dive right in and take a sip.

Raven Ishak

## After my first sip, I honestly... wasn't sure what to make of it. I got a wave of real apples — which was absolutely amazing and almost nostalgia-like (especially if you grew up having a lot of apple juice as a kid) — but I was instantly met with bubbles afterward with no cinnamon spice flavor.
